content:
Hi all,

We are planning to focus our next Rust-for-Linux contribution on nova-core, and we are currently choosing between two task-list items as a first step:

  1. Generic register abstraction (REGA)
  2. FromPrimitive API (FPRI)

As of April 13, 2026, we found prior public discussion around FPRI from the task page links, but we did not find a clearly active public series specifically targeting REGA.

Before going further, we wanted to check publicly to avoid overlap: is either of these already being actively worked on, and if not, would you prefer a first contribution to start with FPRI as the smaller prerequisite, or with REGA if kept tied to immediate nova-core users?

If both are already covered, is there another nova-core task from the public task list that you would recommend as a better first contribution?

Our goal is to keep the work tied to real nova-core users rather than proposing a standalone Rust abstraction.
This topic was moved here from #General > nova-core REGA/FPRI overlap check by Miguel Ojeda.

Hi,

Item 1 is already completed. Item 2 is actively being worked on and nearing completion.

Also at the risk of coming across as unwelcoming and maybe even rude, I would like to ask you to refrain from contributing to nova-core. I have noticed that everything in your patch submissions so far looks 100% LLM-generated, including commit logs and cover-letters (with the required disclosure largely missing), and has resulted in a lot of wasted maintainer time. Even your posts on Zulip appear to be AI-generated.

I just don't have the bandwidth to review (and inevitably reject) AI slop and chat with a bot. Also you are basically using the kernel community as the unwilling test subject of your AI research tool, which I find unethical and offensive.

And don't get me wrong, I don't mind the use of AI to help writing code, comments, or commit logs to some degree. But I also need the human behind it to have a clue about what they are doing, and it doesn't seem to be the case here.

So to be clear, please do not send patches to nova-core.
